  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_0158r6XHptLyHJVRjZZsW1yN",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "text",
        "text": "Perfect! I have all the key files. Let me now provide a comprehensive analysis of the FRG-245 character collision avoidance feature based on my thorough examination of the source code.\n\n## FRG-245 Character Collision Avoidance Feature - Complete Analysis\n\n### 1. Collision Avoidance Methods\n\n**`_getOccupiedTiles(exceptChar)`** (engine.js:411-418)\n```javascript\nReturns: Set<number> - tile keys (ty*MAP_COLS+tx) for all characters except exceptChar\nPurpose: Creates a set of blocked tiles from all other characters' positions\nReturns Set of tile keys where each key = (character.ty * MAP_COLS + character.tx)\n```\n\n**`_moveCharWithCollision(char, goalTx, goalTy)`** (engine.js:421-424)\n```javascript\nSignature: _moveCharWithCollision(char, goalTx, goalTy)\nReturns: boolean - true if path found, false if blocked\nPurpose: Wrapper around char.moveTo() that passes occupied tiles as extraBlocked param\nImplementation:\n  const blocked = this._getOccupiedTiles(char);\n  return char.moveTo(goalTx, goalTy, blocked);\n```\n\n**`_findFreeInteractTile(objPosition, char)`** (engine.js:427-452)\n```javascript\nSignature: _findFreeInteractTile(objPosition, char)\nReturns: { tx: number, ty: number } | null - free tile adjacent to object, or null if none\nPurpose: Finds walkable interaction tile preferring unoccupied tiles\nLogic:\n  1. Gets occupied tiles from other characters\n  2. Finds all adjacent walkable tiles around object\n  3. Returns preferentially from \"free\" (unoccupied) pool\n  4. Falls back to \"all\" pool if no free tiles\n  5. Randomly selects from pool (line 451)\n```\n\n**`_deadlockEscape(char)`** (engine.js:459-476)\n```javascript\nSignature: _deadlockEscape(char)\nReturns: void (moves char to adjacent free tile or clears _pendingTarget if surrounded)\nPurpose: Emergency exit when path is completely blocked after 3 retries\nLogic:\n  1. Tries 4 directions (shuffled): [0,-1], [0,1], [-1,0], [1,0]\n  2. Only moves to walkable, unoccupied tiles\n  3. Preserves _pendingTarget so arrival handler knows to re-path\n  4. If completely surrounded, clears _pendingTarget and _retryCount\nNotes: Does NOT use moveTo() - direct position change via char.moveTo(nx, ny)\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. Character Collision Properties\n\nFrom characters.js (lines 152-155):\n\n```javascript\n// Collision retry state (managed by SimulationEngine)\nthis._pendingTarget = null;  // { obj, action, tile } \u2014 blocked goal awaiting retry\nthis._retryCount = 0;        // number of failed path attempts for current goal\nthis._retryTimer = 0;        // ms until next retry attempt\n```\n\n**Data Structure Details:**\n- `_pendingTarget`: Object with fields: `{ obj, action, tile }`\n  - `obj`: The target object being blocked\n  - `action`: Interaction action to perform ('use', etc.)\n  - `tile`: The free interact tile to reach { tx, ty }\n- `_retryCount`: Integer (0-3) - tracks failed attempts before deadlock escape\n- `_retryTimer`: Number in milliseconds - countdown until next retry attempt\n\n---\n\n### 3. MAP_COLS Export (tilemap.js)\n\n**Export Statement** (tilemap.js:8):\n```javascript\nexport const MAP_COLS = 16;\n```\n\nThe map is 16 tiles wide. Used in:\n- Tile key calculation: `key = ty * MAP_COLS + tx`\n- Bounds checking in `isWalkable(tx, ty)` (line 18)\n- Map array initialization (16 columns per row)\n\n---\n\n### 4. Engine Exposure Pattern (index.html)\n\n**Current Pattern:** The engine is NOT directly exposed on `window._gameEngine`. \n\n**Current Setup** (index.html:256-599):\n- Engine is created as local variable: `let engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });` (line 276)\n- Engine is scoped to the module block (no global exposure)\n- Tests/debugging access engine via local variable in the same script\n\n**To inject window._gameEngine**, the pattern would be (line ~276 area):\n```javascript\nlet engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });\nwindow._gameEngine = engine;  // \u2190 INJECTION POINT\n```\n\nThe code structure shows this is designed to be testable but not yet exposed. No comment marker exists for injection point yet.\n\n---\n\n### 5. snapshot() Method Return Value\n\n**Location:** engine.js:162-179\n\n**Returns Object with these fields:**\n```javascript\n{\n  running: boolean,\n  paused: boolean,\n  elapsedMs: number,\n  tickCount: number,\n  tickIntervalMs: number,\n  timeScale: number,\n  aiMode: boolean,\n  simClock: { \n    day: number, \n    time: string  // e.g., \"8:30 AM\"\n  },\n  objects: [...],  // from objectEngine.snapshot()\n  characters: [\n    {\n      id: string,           // 'maple', 'sol', 'nyx'\n      name: string,\n      tx: number,\n      ty: number,\n      action: string,       // 'idle' | 'walking' | 'interacting'\n      facing: string,       // 'up' | 'down' | 'left' | 'right'\n      mood: string,         // 'happy' | 'content' | 'neutral' | 'upset'\n      targetObjectId: string | null,\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. Injection String Comment Pattern\n\n**Current Status:** No explicit comment marker exists in index.html for `window._gameEngine` injection.\n\nThe closest logical insertion point is **line 276** in the script block:\n```javascript\n// \u2500\u2500 State \u2500\u2500\nlet engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });\n// \u2190 Could add comment here like: window._gameEngine = engine; // Expose for testing/debugging\nlet debugVisible = false;\n```\n\n**Suggested injection comment pattern:**\n```javascript\n// \u2500\u2500 Expose engine for testing/console access \u2500\u2500\n// window._gameEngine = engine;\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. Character Entities\n\n**Character IDs:** All defined in characters.js:323-348\n\n| ID | Name | Sector | Starting Position | Personality |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `maple` | Maple | 'cafe' | (3, 12) | Warm, chatty barista |\n| `sol` | Sol | 'cafe' | (10, 16) | Quiet, bookish regular |\n| `nyx` | Nyx | 'apartment' | (3, 4) | Creative night owl |\n\nAll three are instantiated at engine startup via `CHARACTER_DEFS.map(def => new Character(def))` (engine.js:36).\n\n---\n\n### 8. moveTo() Signature - extraBlocked Parameter\n\n**Location:** characters.js:195-209\n\n```javascript\nmoveTo(goalTx, goalTy, extraBlocked = null) {\n  const path = findPath(\n    Math.round(this.tx), \n    Math.round(this.ty), \n    goalTx, \n    goalTy, \n    extraBlocked  // \u2190 Passed directly to pathfinding\n  );\n  // ... sets path, resets animation, returns boolean\n  if (path && path.length > 0) {\n    this.path = path;\n    this.prevTx = Math.round(this.tx);\n    this.prevTy = Math.round(this.ty);\n    this.moveProgress = 0;\n    this.walkFrame = 0;\n    this.walkTimer = 0;\n    this.action = 'walking';\n    this._updateFacing(path[0].tx - this.prevTx, path[0].ty - this.prevTy);\n    return true;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n```\n\n**Parameter `extraBlocked` Details:**\n- **Type:** `Set<number> | null`\n- **Default:** `null`\n- **Content:** Tile keys (format: `ty * MAP_COLS + tx`) to mark as unwalkable during A* pathfinding\n- **Usage in findPath()** (characters.js:30, line 77):\n  ```javascript\n  // Skip tiles blocked by other characters (but always allow the goal tile)\n  if (extraBlocked && nKey !== goalKey && extraBlocked.has(nKey)) continue;\n```\n- **Key Detail:** Goal tile is ALWAYS allowed even if in extraBlocked (to avoid infinite loops if target is occupied)\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nThe FRG-245 collision avoidance system uses a **retry + deadlock escape** pattern:\n\n1. **Initial pathing** uses `_moveCharWithCollision()` which passes occupied tiles to A*\n2. **If blocked:** Store goal in `_pendingTarget` with `_retryCount=1` and `_retryTimer=500-1000ms`\n3. **On retry timer expiry:** Attempt path 3 times with incrementing retry count\n4. **After 3 failures:** Call `_deadlockEscape()` to move to random adjacent free tile\n5. **After escape:** Arrival handler re-paths to original goal from new position\n6. **Safety:** Goal tile is always pathable even if occupied (prevents deadlock)\n\nAll file paths are absolute in `/home/gem/ai-aquarium-frg-245/`."

   400→        produces: event.produces || null,
   401→        emoji: event.emoji || null,
   402→      });
   403→    } else {
   404→      char.targetObjectId = null;
   405→    }
   406→  }
   407→
   408→  // ── Collision helpers ──
   409→
   410→  /** Returns a Set of tile keys (ty*MAP_COLS+tx) for all characters except `exceptChar`. */
   411→  _getOccupiedTiles(exceptChar) {
   412→    const tiles = new Set();
   413→    for (const c of this.characters) {
   414→      if (c === exceptChar) continue;
   415→      tiles.add(c.ty * MAP_COLS + c.tx);
   416→    }
   417→    return tiles;
   418→  }
   419→
   420→  /** Like char.moveTo but marks other characters' tiles as unwalkable during A*. */
   421→  _moveCharWithCollision(char, goalTx, goalTy) {
   422→    const blocked = this._getOccupiedTiles(char);
   423→    return char.moveTo(goalTx, goalTy, blocked);
   424→  }
   425→
   426→  /** Like findInteractTile but prefers tiles not currently occupied by other characters. */
   427→  _findFreeInteractTile(objPosition, char) {
   428→    const { tx, ty, tw = 1, th = 1 } = objPosition;
   429→    const occupied = this._getOccupiedTiles(char);
   430→    const free = [];
   431→    const all = [];
   432→
   433→    const addCandidate = (x, y) => {
   434→      if (!isWalkable(x, y)) return;
   435→      const entry = { tx: x, ty: y };
   436→      all.push(entry);
   437→      if (!occupied.has(y * MAP_COLS + x)) free.push(entry);
   438→    };
   439→
   440→    for (let x = tx - 1; x <= tx + tw; x++) {
   441→      addCandidate(x, ty - 1);
   442→      addCandidate(x, ty + th);
   443→    }
   444→    for (let y = ty; y < ty + th; y++) {
   445→      addCandidate(tx - 1, y);
   446→      addCandidate(tx + tw, y);
   447→    }
   448→
   449→    const pool = free.length > 0 ? free : all;
   450→    if (pool.length === 0) return null;
   451→    return pool[Math.floor(Math.random() * pool.length)];
   452→  }
   453→
   454→  /**
   455→   * Deadlock escape: move to a random free adjacent tile so the character
   456→   * can re-path around whatever is blocking them. _pendingTarget is preserved
   457→   * so the arrival handler knows to re-path to the original goal.
   458→   */
   459→  _deadlockEscape(char) {
   460→    const DIRS = [[0, -1], [0, 1], [-1, 0], [1, 0]];
   461→    const occupied = this._getOccupiedTiles(char);
   462→    // Shuffle directions for variety
   463→    const dirs = DIRS.slice().sort(() => Math.random() - 0.5);
   464→    for (const [dx, dy] of dirs) {
   465→      const nx = char.tx + dx;
   466→      const ny = char.ty + dy;
   467→      if (isWalkable(nx, ny) && !occupied.has(ny * MAP_COLS + nx)) {
   468→        // Move one tile — _pendingTarget stays set; arrival handler will re-path
   469→        char.moveTo(nx, ny);
   470→        return;
   471→      }
   472→    }
   473→    // Completely surrounded — give up
   474→    char._pendingTarget = null;
   475→    char._retryCount = 0;
   476→  }
   477→
   478→  _getPriorityTarget(char) {
   479→    // Sol: pick up book if closed and nearby
   480→    if (char.id === 'sol') {
   481→      const book = this.objectEngine.get('cafe_book');
   482→      if (book && book.state === 'closed') {
   483→        const dist = Math.abs(book.position.tx - char.tx) + Math.abs(book.position.ty - char.ty);
   484→        if (dist <= 6) {
   485→          return { obj: book, action: 'use' };
   486→        }
   487→      }
   488→    }
   489→
   490→    // Non-Maple near stocked display → take pastry
   491→    if (char.id !== 'maple') {
   492→      const display = this.objectEngine.get('cafe_display');
   493→      if (display && display.state === 'stocked') {
   494→        const dist = Math.abs(display.position.tx - char.tx) + Math.abs(display.position.ty - char.ty);
   495→        if (dist <= 8) {
   496→          return { obj: display, action: 'use' };
   497→        }
   498→      }
   499→    }

x <= tx + tw; x++) {\n   441\u2192      addCandidate(x, ty - 1);\n   442\u2192      addCandidate(x, ty + th);\n   443\u2192    }\n   444\u2192    for (let y = ty; y < ty + th; y++) {\n   445\u2192      addCandidate(tx - 1, y);\n   446\u2192      addCandidate(tx + tw, y);\n   447\u2192    }\n   448\u2192\n   449\u2192    const pool = free.length > 0 ? free : all;\n   450\u2192    if (pool.length === 0) return null;\n   451\u2192    return pool[Math.floor(Math.random() * pool.length)];\n   452\u2192  }\n   453\u2192\n   454\u2192  /**\n   455\u2192   * Deadlock escape: move to a random free adjacent tile so the character\n   456\u2192   * can re-path around whatever is blocking them. _pendingTarget is preserved\n   457\u2192   * so the arrival handler knows to re-path to the original goal.\n   458\u2192   */\n   459\u2192  _deadlockEscape(char) {\n   460\u2192    const DIRS = [[0, -1], [0, 1], [-1, 0], [1, 0]];\n   461\u2192    const occupied = this._getOccupiedTiles(char);\n   462\u2192    // Shuffle directions for variety\n   463\u2192    const dirs = DIRS.slice().sort(() => Math.random() - 0.5);\n   464\u2192    for (const [dx, dy] of dirs) {\n   465\u2192      const nx = char.tx + dx;\n   466\u2192      const ny = char.ty + dy;\n   467\u2192      if (isWalkable(nx, ny) && !occupied.has(ny * MAP_COLS + nx)) {\n   468\u2192        // Move one tile \u2014 _pendingTarget stays set; arrival handler will re-path\n   469\u2192        char.moveTo(nx, ny);\n   470\u2192        return;\n   471\u2192      }\n   472\u2192    }\n   473\u2192    // Completely surrounded \u2014 give up\n   474\u2192    char._pendingTarget = null;\n   475\u2192    char._retryCount = 0;\n   476\u2192  }\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192  _getPriorityTarget(char) {\n   479\u2192    // Sol: pick up book if closed and nearby\n   480\u2192    if (char.id === 'sol') {\n   481\u2192      const book = this.objectEngine.get('cafe_book');\n   482\u2192      if (book && book.state === 'closed') {\n   483\u2192        const dist = Math.abs(book.position.tx - char.tx) + Math.abs(book.position.ty - char.ty);\n   484\u2192        if (dist <= 6) {\n   485\u2192          return { obj: book, action: 'use' };\n   486\u2192        }\n   487\u2192      }\n   488\u2192    }\n   489\u2192\n   490\u2192    // Non-Maple near stocked display \u2192 take pastry\n   491\u2192    if (char.id !== 'maple') {\n   492\u2192      const display = this.objectEngine.get('cafe_display');\n   493\u2192      if (display && display.state === 'stocked') {\n   494\u2192        const dist = Math.abs(display.position.tx - char.tx) + Math.abs(display.position.ty - char.ty);\n   495\u2192        if (dist <= 8) {\n   496\u2192          return { obj: display, action: 'use' };\n   497\u2192        }\n   498\u2192      }\n   499\u2192    }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.    161→  // Full state snapshot
   162→  snapshot() {
   163→    return {
   164→      running: this.running,
   165→      paused: this.paused,
   166→      elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs,
   167→      tickCount: this.tickCount,
   168→      tickIntervalMs: this.tickIntervalMs,
   169→      timeScale: this.timeScale,
   170→      aiMode: this.aiMode,
   171→      simClock: { day: this.simClock.day, time: this.simClock.timeString },
   172→      objects: this.objectEngine.snapshot(),
   173→      characters: this.characters.map(c => ({
   174→        id: c.id, name: c.name, tx: c.tx, ty: c.ty,
   175→        action: c.action, facing: c.facing, mood: c.mood,

   178→    };

   181→  // ── Event bus ──
   182→
   183→  on(eventType, callback) {
   184→    if (!this.listeners.has(eventType)) {
   185→      this.listeners.set(eventType, new Set());
   186→    }
   187→    this.listeners.get(eventType).add(callback);
   188→    return () => this.off(eventType, callback);
   189→  }

USERMESSAGE #78
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   288→  _updateCharacters(deltaMs) {
   289→    for (const char of this.characters) {
   290→      const wasWalking = char.action === 'walking';
   291→      char.update(deltaMs);
   292→
   293→      // Character just arrived at destination
   294→      if (wasWalking && char.action === 'idle') {
   295→        if (char.targetObjectId) {
   296→          const obj = this.objectEngine.get(char.targetObjectId);
   297→          if (obj) {
   298→            this._doInteraction(char, obj, char.pendingAction || 'use');
   299→          } else {
   300→            char.targetObjectId = null;
   301→          }
   302→          char.pendingAction = 'use';
   303→        } else if (char.targetCharId) {
   304→          // Character arrived near another character for conversation — face each other
   305→          const target = this.characters.find(c => c.id === char.targetCharId);
   306→          if (target) {
   307→            char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);
   308→            target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);
   309→          }
   310→          char.targetCharId = null;
   311→        } else if (char._pendingTarget) {
   312→          // Arrived at deadlock-escape tile — try to re-path to the original goal
   313→          const { obj, action, tile } = char._pendingTarget;
   314→          const atTarget = tile.tx === char.tx && tile.ty === char.ty;
   315→          if (atTarget) {
   316→            this._doInteraction(char, obj, action);
   317→            char._pendingTarget = null;
   318→            char._retryCount = 0;
   319→          } else if (this._moveCharWithCollision(char, tile.tx, tile.ty)) {
   320→            char.targetObjectId = obj.id;
   321→            char.pendingAction = action;
   322→            char._pendingTarget = null;
   323→            char._retryCount = 0;
   324→          } else {
   325→            // Still blocked after escape — give up
   326→            char._pendingTarget = null;
   327→            char._retryCount = 0;
   328→          }
   329→        }
   330→      }
   331→
   332→      // Retry timer: character is idle, waiting to re-path to a blocked goal
   333→      if (char.action === 'idle' && char._retryTimer > 0) {
   334→        char._retryTimer -= deltaMs;
   335→        if (char._retryTimer <= 0) {
   336→          char._retryTimer = 0;
   337→          if (char._pendingTarget) {
   338→            const { obj, action, tile } = char._pendingTarget;
   339→            if (this._moveCharWithCollision(char, tile.tx, tile.ty)) {
   340→              // Path found — proceed normally
   341→              char.targetObjectId = obj.id;
   342→              char.pendingAction = action;
   343→              char._pendingTarget = null;
   344→              char._retryCount = 0;
   345→            } else if (char._retryCount < 3) {
   346→              // Still blocked — wait and try again
   347→              char._retryCount++;
   348→              char._retryTimer = 500 + Math.random() * 500;
   349→            } else {
   350→              // 3 failures — escape to an adjacent free tile and re-path from there
   351→              char._retryCount = 0;
   352→              this._deadlockEscape(char);
   353→            }
   354→          }
   355→        }
   356→      }
   357→    }
   358→  }
   359→
   360→  // ── Object → interaction animation mapping ──
   361→  // Maps object ID substrings to the animation played during interaction.
   362→  static _getInteractAnim(objectId) {
   363→    if (!objectId) return null;
   364→    if (objectId.includes('book') || objectId.includes('desk') || objectId.includes('bookshelf')) {
   365→      return 'reading';
   366→    }
   367→    if (objectId.includes('bed') || objectId.includes('kotatsu') || objectId.includes('sofa') || objectId.includes('tv')) {

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #79
Now I have a very clear picture of the engine. Let me write the tests. The injection pattern is: - Route `**/` (catch all) and inject `window._gameEngine = engine;` after `// ── Initial state ──` Let me now write the FRG-245 collision avoidance spec. The key things to test: 1. **Test 1: Characters don't share tiles during walking** - Let sim run 60s, poll positions, verify no two chars on same tile when action is 'idle' or 'interacting' 2. **Test 2: _moveCharWithCollision respects occupied tiles** - Get occupied tiles via engine._getOccupiedTiles(char), try to path through an occupied tile, verify path avoids it, verify char can still reach goal even if occupied 3. **Test 3: Retry and deadlock escape** - Force a retry scenario by setting char._pendingTarget, verify the retry timer counts down, then confirm deadlock escape triggers after three failed attempts 4. **Test 4: _findFreeInteractTile prefers unoccupied tiles** - Place a character on an interact tile and call the function to find a free one, confirming it returns an unoccupied tile 5. **Test 5: Live sim stability** - Run the simulation for 120 seconds and verify characters don't get permanently stuck and the system doesn't crash Now I'll write the spec file on port 5175.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

The injection pattern is:\n- Route `**/` (catch all) and inject `window._gameEngine = engine;` after `// \u2500\u2500 Initial state \u2500\u2500`\n\nLet me now write the FRG-245 collision avoidance spec. The key things to test:\n\n1. **Test 1: Characters don't share tiles during walking** - Let sim run 60s, poll positions, verify no two chars on same tile when action is 'idle' or 'interacting'\n\n2. **Test 2: _moveCharWithCollision respects occupied tiles** - Get occupied tiles via engine._getOccupiedTiles(char), try to path through an occupied tile, verify path avoids it, verify char can still reach goal even if occupied\n   \n3. **Test 3: Retry and deadlock escape** - Force a retry scenario by setting char._pendingTarget, verify the retry timer counts down, then confirm deadlock escape triggers after three failed attempts\n\n4. **Test 4: _findFreeInteractTile prefers unoccupied tiles** - Place a character on an interact tile and call the function to find a free one, confirming it returns an unoccupied tile\n\n5. **Test 5: Live sim stability** - Run the simulation for 120 seconds and verify characters don't get permanently stuck and the system doesn't crash\n\nNow I'll write the spec file on port 5175.",

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #93
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/collision-avoidance.spec.js",
  "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-245\n * Character-to-character collision avoidance.\n *\n * Tests run against the local dev server (http://localhost:5175) which serves\n * the feature/frg-245-character-collision branch.\n *\n * The page does NOT expose window._gameEngine by default, so we intercept\n * index.html via page.route() and inject the exposure before the script executes.\n *\n * Test layout:\n *   1. No tile sharing \u2014 no two characters occupy the same tile when idle/interacting\n *   2. _moveCharWithCollision respects occupied tiles and preserves goal exemption\n *   3. Retry and deadlock escape \u2014 _retryTimer counts down, _deadlockEscape fires at 3 failures\n *   4. _findFreeInteractTile prefers unoccupied tiles when alternatives exist\n *   5. Live sim stability \u2014 no permanently stuck characters over 120s of sim time\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n\nconst LOCAL_URL = 'http://localhost:5175';\nconst MAP_COLS = 16; // from tilemap.js \u2014 width of tile grid\n\n// \u2500\u2500 helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/**\n * Intercept index.html and inject window._gameEngine = engine so tests can\n * call page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.*).\n */\nasync function injectEngineExposure(page) {\n  await page.route('**/', async (route) => {\n    const response = await route.fetch();\n    let body = await response.text();\n    body = body.replace(\n      '// \u2500\u2500 Initial state \u2500\u2500',\n      '// \u2500\u2500 Initial state (QA injection) \u2500\u2500\\nwindow._gameEngine = engine;'\n    );\n    await route.fulfill({ response, body });\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Click start overlay (if visible) and optionally boost timeScale.\n */\nasync function startEngine(page, timeScale = 1) {\n  const overlay = page.locator('#start-overlay');\n  if (await overlay.isVisible()) {\n    await overlay.click();\n  }\n  if (timeScale !== 1) {\n    await page.evaluate((scale) => {\n      if (window._gameEngine) window._gameEngine.timeScale = scale;\n    }, timeScale);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Poll engine snapshot until predicate returns truthy or timeout.\n */\nasync function pollSnapshot(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 45_000, intervalMs = 300) {\n  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\n  while (Date.now() < deadline) {\n    const snap = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.snapshot());\n    if (predicate(snap)) return snap;\n    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);\n  }\n  throw new Error(`pollSnapshot timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest.describe('FRG-245: Character Collision Avoidance', () => {\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 1: No tile sharing during idle/interacting \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('no two characters share a tile when idle or interacting over 60s', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n    await startEngine(page, 20); // 20x speed so 60 sim-seconds = ~3 real seconds\n\n    const violations = [];\n    const POLL_COUNT = 30;\n    const POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 300; // ~9 real seconds at 20x = 180 sim-seconds\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < POLL_COUNT; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n\n      const charPositions = await page.evaluate(() =>\n        window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n          id: c.id,\n          tx: Math.round(c.tx),\n          ty: Math.round(c.ty),\n          action: c.action,\n        }))\n      );\n\n      // Only check tile sharing when characters are in a stable state (idle/interacting)\n      const stable = charPositions.filter(c => c.action === 'idle' || c.action === 'interacting');\n\n      for (let a = 0; a < stable.length; a++) {\n        for (let b = a + 1; b < stable.length; b++) {\n          if (stable[a].tx === stable[b].tx && stable[a].ty === stable[b].ty) {\n            violations.push({\n              poll: i,\n              charA: stable[a].id,\n              charB: stable[b].id,\n              tile: `(${stable[a].tx},${stable[a].ty})`,\n              actions: `${stable[a].action}/${stable[b].action}`,\n            });\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (violations.length > 0) {\n      console.error('Tile-sharing violations:', JSON.stringify(violations, null, 2));\n    }\n\n    expect(violations.length,\n      `Characters shared tiles on ${violations.length} poll(s): ` +\n      violations.map(v => `${v.charA}+${v.charB}@${v.tile}`).join(', ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 2: _moveCharWithCollision respects occupied tiles \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_moveCharWithCollision avoids occupied tiles but still reaches goal', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate((MAP_COLS) => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Place maple as a blocker mid-path\n      maple.tx = 7; maple.ty = 4;\n\n      // Place nyx as the mover \u2014 wants to reach (10, 4) but maple is at (7, 4)\n      nyx.tx = 4; nyx.ty = 4;\n      nyx.prevTx = 4; nyx.prevTy = 4;\n      nyx.action = 'idle';\n      nyx.path = [];\n      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n\n      const blockerKey = maple.ty * MAP_COLS + maple.tx;\n\n      // Move with collision \u2014 should avoid maple's tile\n      const success = engine._moveCharWithCollision(nyx, 10, 4);\n\n      if (!success) {\n        return { success: false, pathKeys: [], blockerKey, blockerOnPath: false };\n      }\n\n      const pathKeys = nyx.path.map(step => step.ty * MAP_COLS + step.tx);\n      const blockerOnPath = pathKeys.includes(blockerKey);\n\n      return {\n        success,\n        pathKeys,\n        blockerKey,\n        blockerOnPath,\n        pathLength: nyx.path.length,\n      };\n    }, MAP_COLS);\n\n    console.log('_moveCharWithCollision result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.success, 'Should find a path even with blocker in the way').toBe(true);\n    expect(result.blockerOnPath,\n      `Path must not route through blocker tile (key ${result.blockerKey})`\n    ).toBe(false);\n    expect(result.pathLength, 'Path should not be empty').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n  });\n\n  test('_moveCharWithCollision allows pathing to a tile even if it is occupied (goal exemption)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate((MAP_COLS) => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Place maple directly on the goal tile\n      maple.tx = 10; maple.ty = 4;\n\n      // Nyx wants to reach (10, 4) \u2014 the tile occupied by maple\n      nyx.tx = 4; nyx.ty = 4;\n      nyx.prevTx = 4; nyx.prevTy = 4;\n      nyx.action = 'idle';\n      nyx.path = [];\n      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n\n      // _moveCharWithCollision should still allow goal tile even if occupied\n      const success = engine._moveCharWithCollision(nyx, 10, 4);\n\n      return {\n        success,\n        goalIsOccupied: true, // we know it is: maple.tx=10, maple.ty=4\n        pathLength: nyx.path.length,\n      };\n    }, MAP_COLS);\n\n    console.log('Goal-exemption result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.success,\n      'Should be able to path to an occupied goal tile (goal exemption must hold)'\n    ).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 3: Retry and deadlock escape \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_retryTimer counts down and _retryCount increments on repeated failures', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Build a fake blocked-path scenario:\n      // Nyx is idle with a _pendingTarget; _moveCharWithCollision will fail because\n      // we pack all other characters onto the only viable path tile.\n\n      // Place nyx in a corner-ish spot\n      nyx.tx = 1; nyx.ty = 12;\n      nyx.prevTx = 1; nyx.prevTy = 12;\n      nyx.action = 'idle';\n      nyx.path = [];\n      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n\n      // Block the path to tile (2, 11) by placing sol and maple on adjacent tiles\n      sol.tx = 2; sol.ty = 12;\n      maple.tx = 1; maple.ty = 11;\n\n      const fakeObj = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');\n      // Set up a _pendingTarget pointing to a blocked tile\n      nyx._pendingTarget = { obj: fakeObj, action: 'use', tile: { tx: 3, ty: 11 } };\n      nyx._retryCount = 1;\n      nyx._retryTimer = 800; // 800ms remaining\n\n      const before = {\n        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,\n        retryTimer: nyx._retryTimer,\n        hasPendingTarget: !!nyx._pendingTarget,\n      };\n\n      // Advance 400ms \u2014 timer should partially count down but not fire yet\n      engine._updateCharacters(400);\n\n      const midpoint = {\n        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,\n        retryTimer: nyx._retryTimer,\n        hasPendingTarget: !!nyx._pendingTarget,\n      };\n\n      return { before, midpoint };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Retry timer test:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.before.retryTimer, 'Timer should start at 800ms').toBe(800);\n    expect(result.midpoint.retryTimer,\n      'After 400ms, timer should have decreased by 400ms'\n    ).toBeLessThan(800);\n    expect(result.midpoint.retryTimer,\n      'Timer should still be > 0 (400ms of 800ms elapsed)'\n    ).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    expect(result.midpoint.hasPendingTarget,\n      '_pendingTarget should still be set (timer has not fired yet)'\n    ).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  test('_deadlockEscape fires after 3 failed retries and moves char to adjacent free tile', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Place nyx in the caf\u00e9 sector with an open adjacency for escape\n      nyx.tx = 8; nyx.ty = 14;\n      nyx.prevTx = 8; nyx.prevTy = 14;\n      nyx.action = 'idle';\n      nyx.path = [];\n      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n\n      // Block all neighbours EXCEPT one, to force a specific escape direction.\n      // Adjacent tiles to (8,14): (8,13),(8,15),(7,14),(9,14)\n      // Leave (9,14) free, block the rest with sol and maple\n      sol.tx = 8; sol.ty = 13;\n      maple.tx = 7; maple.ty = 14;\n      // (8,15) is the floor below \u2014 check if it's walkable (it may be a wall)\n      // For this test it doesn't matter \u2014 escape picks any free walkable tile.\n\n      const fakeObj = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');\n      // Set retryCount = 3 so next expiry triggers _deadlockEscape\n      nyx._pendingTarget = { obj: fakeObj, action: 'use', tile: { tx: 5, ty: 11 } };\n      nyx._retryCount = 3;\n      nyx._retryTimer = 10; // almost expired\n\n      const before = { tx: nyx.tx, ty: nyx.ty, retryCount: nyx._retryCount };\n\n      // Advance enough to fire the timer (10ms) and trigger escape\n      engine._updateCharacters(50);\n\n      const after = {\n        tx: Math.round(nyx.tx),\n        ty: Math.round(nyx.ty),\n        action: nyx.action,\n        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,\n      };\n\n      const moved = after.tx !== before.tx || after.ty !== before.ty;\n\n      return { before, after, moved };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Deadlock escape test:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    // After deadlock escape, character should have moved OR _pendingTarget was cleared (surrounded)\n    // Either outcome is valid: escape moved char, or gave up cleanly.\n    const escapedOrCleared = result.moved || result.after.retryCount === 0;\n    expect(escapedOrCleared,\n      'After 3 retries: character should escape to adjacent tile OR _retryCount reset to 0'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    if (result.moved) {\n      const dist = Math.abs(result.after.tx - result.before.tx) + Math.abs(result.after.ty - result.before.ty);\n      expect(dist, 'Escape should move exactly 1 tile (Manhattan distance)').toBeLessThanOrEqual(1);\n    }\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 4: _findFreeInteractTile prefers unoccupied tiles \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_findFreeInteractTile returns an unoccupied tile when alternatives exist', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    // Run multiple trials \u2014 the function is random, so we sample several times\n    const TRIALS = 20;\n    const result = await page.evaluate((TRIALS) => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Use cafe_espresso at (tx=2, ty=11) \u2014 single tile\n      const espresso = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');\n      if (!espresso) return { error: 'cafe_espresso not found' };\n\n      // Occupy ONE interact tile around espresso with maple.\n      // Adjacent walkable tiles around (2,11): typically (1,11),(3,11),(2,10),(2,12)\n      // We'll park maple at (1,11) \u2014 one of the interact tiles.\n      maple.tx = 1; maple.ty = 11;\n\n      // Nyx asks for a free interact tile \u2014 maple's tile should not be returned\n      // (because at least one other candidate tile exists)\n      const mapleKey = maple.ty * 16 + maple.tx; // MAP_COLS=16\n      const trials = [];\n\n      for (let i = 0; i < TRIALS; i++) {\n        const tile = engine._findFreeInteractTile(espresso.position, nyx);\n        if (!tile) {\n          trials.push({ error: 'null' });\n          continue;\n        }\n        const key = tile.ty * 16 + tile.tx;\n        trials.push({ tx: tile.tx, ty: tile.ty, isMaple: key === mapleKey });\n      }\n\n      return { trials, maplePos: { tx: maple.tx, ty: maple.ty }, objPos: espresso.position };\n    }, TRIALS);\n\n    if (result.error) {\n      console.log('[SKIP]', result.error);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    console.log('_findFreeInteractTile trials:', JSON.stringify(result.trials));\n\n    const nullCount = result.trials.filter(t => t.error === 'null').length;\n    const mapleCount = result.trials.filter(t => t.isMaple).length;\n\n    expect(nullCount,\n      `_findFreeInteractTile should not return null when adjacents exist (got ${nullCount}/${TRIALS} nulls)`\n    ).toBe(0);\n\n    expect(mapleCount,\n      `_findFreeInteractTile should never return maple's occupied tile when free alternatives exist ` +\n      `(returned maple's tile ${mapleCount}/${TRIALS} times)`\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: Live sim stability over 120s \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('no character gets permanently stuck and sim stays stable over 120s', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(180_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n    await startEngine(page, 20); // 20x speed \u2192 120 sim-seconds \u2248 6 real seconds\n\n    // Drive 120 sim-seconds by advancing engine directly in a fast loop.\n    // We take periodic snapshots to detect stuck characters (same tile, idle, no movement).\n    const stability = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const STEP_MS = 500;          // 0.5 sim-second steps\n      const TOTAL_SIM_MS = 120_000; // 120 sim-seconds\n      const STEPS = TOTAL_SIM_MS / STEP_MS;\n\n      const lastIdleSince = {};    // charId \u2192 sim-ms when they became idle at current tile\n      const lastPos = {};          // charId \u2192 { tx, ty }\n      const stuckEvents = [];      // characters idle > 30s at same tile\n      let simMs = 0;\n\n      for (const c of engine.characters) {\n        lastPos[c.id] = { tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty) };\n        lastIdleSince[c.id] = 0;\n      }\n\n      let errors = [];\n      for (let step = 0; step < STEPS; step++) {\n        try {\n          engine._updateCharacters(STEP_MS);\n        } catch (e) {\n          errors.push({ step, simMs, msg: String(e) });\n          break;\n        }\n        simMs += STEP_MS;\n\n        for (const c of engine.characters) {\n          const tx = Math.round(c.tx);\n          const ty = Math.round(c.ty);\n\n          if (c.action !== 'idle') {\n            // Moving or interacting \u2014 not stuck\n            lastPos[c.id] = { tx, ty };\n            lastIdleSince[c.id] = simMs;\n            continue;\n          }\n\n          // Idle: check if position changed\n          if (tx !== lastPos[c.id].tx || ty !== lastPos[c.id].ty) {\n            lastPos[c.id] = { tx, ty };\n            lastIdleSince[c.id] = simMs;\n          }\n\n          const idleDuration = simMs - lastIdleSince[c.id];\n          if (idleDuration > 30_000) {\n            stuckEvents.push({ id: c.id, tx, ty, idleDuration, simMs });\n            // Reset so we don't spam the same stuck event\n            lastIdleSince[c.id] = simMs;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      return { errors, stuckEvents, simMs };\n    });\n\n    console.log(`Sim ran for ${stability.simMs}ms sim-time`);\n    if (stability.errors.length > 0) console.error('Sim errors:', stability.errors);\n    if (stability.stuckEvents.length > 0) console.warn('Stuck events:', stability.stuckEvents);\n\n    expect(stability.errors.length,\n      `Sim threw ${stability.errors.length} error(s): ` +\n      stability.errors.map(e => e.msg).join('; ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n\n    expect(stability.stuckEvents.length,\n      `${stability.stuckEvents.length} character(s) were stuck for >30s: ` +\n      stability.stuckEvents.map(e => `${e.id}@(${e.tx},${e.ty}) for ${e.idleDuration}ms`).join(', ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n});\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_013D1TLjj9Lm1kiq6aAHSgk9",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/collision-avoidance.spec.js",
      "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-245\n * Character-to-character collision avoidance.\n *\n * Tests run against the local dev server (http://localhost:5175) which serves\n * the feature/frg-245-character-collision branch.\n *\n * The page does NOT expose window._gameEngine by default, so we intercept\n * index.html via page.route() and inject the exposure before the script executes.\n *\n * Test layout:\n *   1. No tile sharing \u2014 no two characters occupy the same tile when idle/interacting\n *   2. _moveCharWithCollision respects occupied tiles and preserves goal exemption\n *   3. Retry and deadlock escape \u2014 _retryTimer counts down, _deadlockEscape fires at 3 failures\n *   4. _findFreeInteractTile prefers unoccupied tiles when alternatives exist\n *   5. Live sim stability \u2014 no permanently stuck characters over 120s of sim time\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n\nconst LOCAL_URL = 'http://localhost:5175';\nconst MAP_COLS = 16; // from tilemap.js \u2014 width of tile grid\n\n// \u2500\u2500 helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/**\n * Intercept index.html and inject window._gameEngine = engine so tests can\n * call page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.*).\n */\nasync function injectEngineExposure(page) {\n  await page.route('**/', async (route) => {\n    const response = await route.fetch();\n    let body = await response.text();\n    body = body.replace(\n      '// \u2500\u2500 Initial state \u2500\u2500',\n      '// \u2500\u2500 Initial state (QA injection) \u2500\u2500\\nwindow._gameEngine = engine;'\n    );\n    await route.fulfill({ response, body });\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Click start overlay (if visible) and optionally boost timeScale.\n */\nasync function startEngine(page, timeScale = 1) {\n  const overlay = page.locator('#start-overlay');\n  if (await overlay.isVisible()) {\n    await overlay.click();\n  }\n  if (timeScale !== 1) {\n    await page.evaluate((scale) => {\n      if (window._gameEngine) window._gameEngine.timeScale = scale;\n    }, timeScale);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Poll engine snapshot until predicate returns truthy or timeout.\n */\nasync function pollSnapshot(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 45_000, intervalMs = 300) {\n  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\n  while (Date.now() < deadline) {\n    const snap = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.snapshot());\n    if (predicate(snap)) return snap;\n    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);\n  }\n  throw new Error(`pollSnapshot timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest.describe('FRG-245: Character Collision Avoidance', () => {\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 1: No tile sharing during idle/interacting \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('no two characters share a tile when idle or interacting over 60s', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n    await startEngine(page, 20); // 20x speed so 60 sim-seconds = ~3 real seconds\n\n    const violations = [];\n    const POLL_COUNT = 30;\n    const POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 300; // ~9 real seconds at 20x = 180 sim-seconds\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < POLL_COUNT; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n\n      const charPositions = await page.evaluate(() =>\n        window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n          id: c.id,\n          tx: Math.round(c.tx),\n          ty: Math.round(c.ty),\n          action: c.action,\n        }))\n      );\n\n      // Only check tile sharing when characters are in a stable state (idle/interacting)\n      const stable = charPositions.filter(c => c.action === 'idle' || c.action === 'interacting');\n\n      for (let a = 0; a < stable.length; a++) {\n        for (let b = a + 1; b < stable.length; b++) {\n          if (stable[a].tx === stable[b].tx && stable[a].ty === stable[b].ty) {\n            violations.push({\n              poll: i,\n              charA: stable[a].id,\n              charB: stable[b].id,\n              tile: `(${stable[a].tx},${stable[a].ty})`,\n              actions: `${stable[a].action}/${stable[b].action}`,\n            });\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (violations.length > 0) {\n      console.error('Tile-sharing violations:', JSON.stringify(violations, null, 2));\n    }\n\n    expect(violations.length,\n      `Characters shared tiles on ${violations.length} poll(s): ` +\n      violations.map(v => `${v.charA}+${v.charB}@${v.tile}`).join(', ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 2: _moveCharWithCollision respects occupied tiles \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_moveCharWithCollision avoids occupied tiles but still reaches goal', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate((MAP_COLS) => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Place maple as a blocker mid-path\n      maple.tx = 7; maple.ty = 4;\n\n      // Place nyx as the mover \u2014 wants to reach (10, 4) but maple is at (7, 4)\n      nyx.tx = 4; nyx.ty = 4;\n      nyx.prevTx = 4; nyx.prevTy = 4;\n      nyx.action = 'idle';\n      nyx.path = [];\n      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n\n      const blockerKey = maple.ty * MAP_COLS + maple.tx;\n\n      // Move with collision \u2014 should avoid maple's tile\n      const success = engine._moveCharWithCollision(nyx, 10, 4);\n\n      if (!success) {\n        return { success: false, pathKeys: [], blockerKey, blockerOnPath: false };\n      }\n\n      const pathKeys = nyx.path.map(step => step.ty * MAP_COLS + step.tx);\n      const blockerOnPath = pathKeys.includes(blockerKey);\n\n      return {\n        success,\n        pathKeys,\n        blockerKey,\n        blockerOnPath,\n        pathLength: nyx.path.length,\n      };\n    }, MAP_COLS);\n\n    console.log('_moveCharWithCollision result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.success, 'Should find a path even with blocker in the way').toBe(true);\n    expect(result.blockerOnPath,\n      `Path must not route through blocker tile (key ${result.blockerKey})`\n    ).toBe(false);\n    expect(result.pathLength, 'Path should not be empty').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n  });\n\n  test('_moveCharWithCollision allows pathing to a tile even if it is occupied (goal exemption)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate((MAP_COLS) => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Place maple directly on the goal tile\n      maple.tx = 10; maple.ty = 4;\n\n      // Nyx wants to reach (10, 4) \u2014 the tile occupied by maple\n      nyx.tx = 4; nyx.ty = 4;\n      nyx.prevTx = 4; nyx.prevTy = 4;\n      nyx.action = 'idle';\n      nyx.path = [];\n      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n\n      // _moveCharWithCollision should still allow goal tile even if occupied\n      const success = engine._moveCharWithCollision(nyx, 10, 4);\n\n      return {\n        success,\n        goalIsOccupied: true, // we know it is: maple.tx=10, maple.ty=4\n        pathLength: nyx.path.length,\n      };\n    }, MAP_COLS);\n\n    console.log('Goal-exemption result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.success,\n      'Should be able to path to an occupied goal tile (goal exemption must hold)'\n    ).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 3: Retry and deadlock escape \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_retryTimer counts down and _retryCount increments on repeated failures', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Build a fake blocked-path scenario:\n      // Nyx is idle with a _pendingTarget; _moveCharWithCollision will fail because\n      // we pack all other characters onto the only viable path tile.\n\n      // Place nyx in a corner-ish spot\n      nyx.tx = 1; nyx.ty = 12;\n      nyx.prevTx = 1; nyx.prevTy = 12;\n      nyx.action = 'idle';\n      nyx.path = [];\n      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n\n      // Block the path to tile (2, 11) by placing sol and maple on adjacent tiles\n      sol.tx = 2; sol.ty = 12;\n      maple.tx = 1; maple.ty = 11;\n\n      const fakeObj = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');\n      // Set up a _pendingTarget pointing to a blocked tile\n      nyx._pendingTarget = { obj: fakeObj, action: 'use', tile: { tx: 3, ty: 11 } };\n      nyx._retryCount = 1;\n      nyx._retryTimer = 800; // 800ms remaining\n\n      const before = {\n        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,\n        retryTimer: nyx._retryTimer,\n        hasPendingTarget: !!nyx._pendingTarget,\n      };\n\n      // Advance 400ms \u2014 timer should partially count down but not fire yet\n      engine._updateCharacters(400);\n\n      const midpoint = {\n        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,\n        retryTimer: nyx._retryTimer,\n        hasPendingTarget: !!nyx._pendingTarget,\n      };\n\n      return { before, midpoint };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Retry timer test:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.before.retryTimer, 'Timer should start at 800ms').toBe(800);\n    expect(result.midpoint.retryTimer,\n      'After 400ms, timer should have decreased by 400ms'\n    ).toBeLessThan(800);\n    expect(result.midpoint.retryTimer,\n      'Timer should still be > 0 (400ms of 800ms elapsed)'\n    ).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    expect(result.midpoint.hasPendingTarget,\n      '_pendingTarget should still be set (timer has not fired yet)'\n    ).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  test('_deadlockEscape fires after 3 failed retries and moves char to adjacent free tile', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Place nyx in the caf\u00e9 sector with an open adjacency for escape\n      nyx.tx = 8; nyx.ty = 14;\n      nyx.prevTx = 8; nyx.prevTy = 14;\n      nyx.action = 'idle';\n      nyx.path = [];\n      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n\n      // Block all neighbours EXCEPT one, to force a specific escape direction.\n      // Adjacent tiles to (8,14): (8,13),(8,15),(7,14),(9,14)\n      // Leave (9,14) free, block the rest with sol and maple\n      sol.tx = 8; sol.ty = 13;\n      maple.tx = 7; maple.ty = 14;\n      // (8,15) is the floor below \u2014 check if it's walkable (it may be a wall)\n      // For this test it doesn't matter \u2014 escape picks any free walkable tile.\n\n      const fakeObj = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');\n      // Set retryCount = 3 so next expiry triggers _deadlockEscape\n      nyx._pendingTarget = { obj: fakeObj, action: 'use', tile: { tx: 5, ty: 11 } };\n      nyx._retryCount = 3;\n      nyx._retryTimer = 10; // almost expired\n\n      const before = { tx: nyx.tx, ty: nyx.ty, retryCount: nyx._retryCount };\n\n      // Advance enough to fire the timer (10ms) and trigger escape\n      engine._updateCharacters(50);\n\n      const after = {\n        tx: Math.round(nyx.tx),\n        ty: Math.round(nyx.ty),\n        action: nyx.action,\n        retryCount: nyx._retryCount,\n      };\n\n      const moved = after.tx !== before.tx || after.ty !== before.ty;\n\n      return { before, after, moved };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Deadlock escape test:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    // After deadlock escape, character should have moved OR _pendingTarget was cleared (surrounded)\n    // Either outcome is valid: escape moved char, or gave up cleanly.\n    const escapedOrCleared = result.moved || result.after.retryCount === 0;\n    expect(escapedOrCleared,\n      'After 3 retries: character should escape to adjacent tile OR _retryCount reset to 0'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    if (result.moved) {\n      const dist = Math.abs(result.after.tx - result.before.tx) + Math.abs(result.after.ty - result.before.ty);\n      expect(dist, 'Escape should move exactly 1 tile (Manhattan distance)').toBeLessThanOrEqual(1);\n    }\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 4: _findFreeInteractTile prefers unoccupied tiles \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_findFreeInteractTile returns an unoccupied tile when alternatives exist', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    // Run multiple trials \u2014 the function is random, so we sample several times\n    const TRIALS = 20;\n    const result = await page.evaluate((TRIALS) => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const [maple, sol, nyx] = engine.characters;\n\n      // Use cafe_espresso at (tx=2, ty=11) \u2014 single tile\n      const espresso = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');\n      if (!espresso) return { error: 'cafe_espresso not found' };\n\n      // Occupy ONE interact tile around espresso with maple.\n      // Adjacent walkable tiles around (2,11): typically (1,11),(3,11),(2,10),(2,12)\n      // We'll park maple at (1,11) \u2014 one of the interact tiles.\n      maple.tx = 1; maple.ty = 11;\n\n      // Nyx asks for a free interact tile \u2014 maple's tile should not be returned\n      // (because at least one other candidate tile exists)\n      const mapleKey = maple.ty * 16 + maple.tx; // MAP_COLS=16\n      const trials = [];\n\n      for (let i = 0; i < TRIALS; i++) {\n        const tile = engine._findFreeInteractTile(espresso.position, nyx);\n        if (!tile) {\n          trials.push({ error: 'null' });\n          continue;\n        }\n        const key = tile.ty * 16 + tile.tx;\n        trials.push({ tx: tile.tx, ty: tile.ty, isMaple: key === mapleKey });\n      }\n\n      return { trials, maplePos: { tx: maple.tx, ty: maple.ty }, objPos: espresso.position };\n    }, TRIALS);\n\n    if (result.error) {\n      console.log('[SKIP]', result.error);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    console.log('_findFreeInteractTile trials:', JSON.stringify(result.trials));\n\n    const nullCount = result.trials.filter(t => t.error === 'null').length;\n    const mapleCount = result.trials.filter(t => t.isMaple).length;\n\n    expect(nullCount,\n      `_findFreeInteractTile should not return null when adjacents exist (got ${nullCount}/${TRIALS} nulls)`\n    ).toBe(0);\n\n    expect(mapleCount,\n      `_findFreeInteractTile should never return maple's occupied tile when free alternatives exist ` +\n      `(returned maple's tile ${mapleCount}/${TRIALS} times)`\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: Live sim stability over 120s \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('no character gets permanently stuck and sim stays stable over 120s', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(180_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n    await startEngine(page, 20); // 20x speed \u2192 120 sim-seconds \u2248 6 real seconds\n\n    // Drive 120 sim-seconds by advancing engine directly in a fast loop.\n    // We take periodic snapshots to detect stuck characters (same tile, idle, no movement).\n    const stability = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const STEP_MS = 500;          // 0.5 sim-second steps\n      const TOTAL_SIM_MS = 120_000; // 120 sim-seconds\n      const STEPS = TOTAL_SIM_MS / STEP_MS;\n\n      const lastIdleSince = {};    // charId \u2192 sim-ms when they became idle at current tile\n      const lastPos = {};          // charId \u2192 { tx, ty }\n      const stuckEvents = [];      // characters idle > 30s at same tile\n      let simMs = 0;\n\n      for (const c of engine.characters) {\n        lastPos[c.id] = { tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty) };\n        lastIdleSince[c.id] = 0;\n      }\n\n      let errors = [];\n      for (let step = 0; step < STEPS; step++) {\n        try {\n          engine._updateCharacters(STEP_MS);\n        } catch (e) {\n          errors.push({ step, simMs, msg: String(e) });\n          break;\n        }\n        simMs += STEP_MS;\n\n        for (const c of engine.characters) {\n          const tx = Math.round(c.tx);\n          const ty = Math.round(c.ty);\n\n          if (c.action !== 'idle') {\n            // Moving or interacting \u2014 not stuck\n            lastPos[c.id] = { tx, ty };\n            lastIdleSince[c.id] = simMs;\n            continue;\n          }\n\n          // Idle: check if position changed\n          if (tx !== lastPos[c.id].tx || ty !== lastPos[c.id].ty) {\n            lastPos[c.id] = { tx, ty };\n            lastIdleSince[c.id] = simMs;\n          }\n\n          const idleDuration = simMs - lastIdleSince[c.id];\n          if (idleDuration > 30_000) {\n            stuckEvents.push({ id: c.id, tx, ty, idleDuration, simMs });\n            // Reset so we don't spam the same stuck event\n            lastIdleSince[c.id] = simMs;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      return { errors, stuckEvents, simMs };\n    });\n\n    console.log(`Sim ran for ${stability.simMs}ms sim-time`);\n    if (stability.errors.length > 0) console.error('Sim errors:', stability.errors);\n    if (stability.stuckEvents.length > 0) console.warn('Stuck events:', stability.stuckEvents);\n\n    expect(stability.errors.length,\n      `Sim threw ${stability.errors.length} error(s): ` +\n      stability.errors.map(e => e.msg).join('; ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n\n    expect(stability.stuckEvents.length,\n      `${stability.stuckEvents.length} character(s) were stuck for >30s: ` +\n      stability.stuckEvents.map(e => `${e.id}@(${e.tx},${e.ty}) for ${e.idleDuration}ms`).join(', ')\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #99
The test 5 (long-running stability) is failing. The issue is that when driving the sim via `_updateCharacters()` only (not the full RAF loop), the AI engine that generates new tasks for characters isn't being called. The characters start at their initial spawn positions and stay idle forever because the AI/decision-making loop isn't triggered. Looking at the engine: - `_updateCharacters()` handles movement updates (walking -> idle transitions) - But the AI task scheduling and character decisions seem to happen elsewhere Let me look at how the engine ticks:

   100→    }
   101→    this._emit('engine:stop', { elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs, tickCount: this.tickCount });
   102→  }
   103→
   104→  pause() {
   105→    if (!this.running) return;
   106→    this.paused = true;
   107→    this._emit('engine:pause', { elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs });
   108→  }
   109→
   110→  resume() {
   111→    if (!this.running || !this.paused) return;
   112→    this.paused = false;
   113→    this._lastTimestamp = performance.now();
   114→    this._emit('engine:resume', { elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs });
   115→  }
   116→
   117→  // Manually advance by a number of ticks (useful for testing)
   118→  manualTick(count = 1) {
   119→    const events = [];
   120→    for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {
   121→      const tickEvents = this.objectEngine.tick(this.tickIntervalMs);
   122→      this.tickCount++;
   123→      this.elapsedMs += this.tickIntervalMs;
   124→      this._wanderTick();
   125→      this._updateCharacters(this.tickIntervalMs);
   126→      events.push(...tickEvents);
   127→    }
   128→    this._emit('engine:tick', {
   129→      tickCount: this.tickCount,
   130→      elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs,
   131→      events,
   132→    });
   133→    return events;
   134→  }
   135→
   136→  // Interact with an object (character-triggered)
   137→  interact(objectId, action = 'use') {
   138→    return this.objectEngine.interact(objectId, action);
   139→  }
   140→
   141→  // Get object by id
   142→  getObject(objectId) {
   143→    return this.objectEngine.get(objectId);
   144→  }
   145→
   146→  // Get all objects
   147→  getAllObjects() {
   148→    return this.objectEngine.getAll();
   149→  }
   150→
   151→  // Get objects in a sector
   152→  getObjectsInSector(sector) {
   153→    return this.objectEngine.getBySector(sector);
   154→  }
   155→
   156→  // Get all characters
   157→  getCharacters() {
   158→    return this.characters;
   159→  }

i < count; i++) {\n   121\u2192      const tickEvents = this.objectEngine.tick(this.tickIntervalMs);\n   122\u2192      this.tickCount++;\n   123\u2192      this.elapsedMs += this.tickIntervalMs;\n   124\u2192      this._wanderTick();\n   125\u2192      this._updateCharacters(this.tickIntervalMs);\n   126\u2192      events.push(...tickEvents);\n   127\u2192    }\n   128\u2192    this._emit('engine:tick', {\n   129\u2192      tickCount: this.tickCount,\n   130\u2192      elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs,\n   131\u2192      events,\n   132\u2192    });\n   133\u2192    return events;\n   134\u2192  }\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192  // Interact with an object (character-triggered)\n   137\u2192  interact(objectId, action = 'use') {\n   138\u2192    return this.objectEngine.interact(objectId, action);\n   139\u2192  }\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192  // Get object by id\n   142\u2192  getObject(objectId) {\n   143\u2192    return this.objectEngine.get(objectId);\n   144\u2192  }\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192  // Get all objects\n   147\u2192  getAllObjects() {\n   148\u2192    return this.objectEngine.getAll();\n   149\u2192  }\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  // Get objects in a sector\n   152\u2192  getObjectsInSector(sector) {\n   153\u2192    return this.objectEngine.getBySector(sector);\n   154\u2192  }\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  // Get all characters\n   157\u2192  getCharacters() {\n   158\u2192    return this.characters;\n   159\u2192  }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. ASSISTANTMESSAGE #107
I see — the tick interval is 5000ms by default. So `manualTick(1)` advances 5000ms of sim-time at a time. The issue with my stability test is that I'm only calling `_updateCharacters(500ms)` which doesn't trigger the wander logic. I should use `manualTick()` instead, which advances 5000ms per tick (including wander decisions). Also, the wander cooldown starts at a large value so characters need to receive ticks to start wandering. Let me fix the test to use `manualTick()`. Actually, looking more carefully: the issue is all characters start at their initial positions (spawn positions: maple at 3,12, sol at 10,16, nyx at 3,4) and need the `_wanderTick()` to fire to get them moving. For a 120 second test, I need 24 ticks (120000ms ÷ 5000ms per tick), which should give the wander cooldown enough time to trigger and get characters moving.

USERMESSAGE #109
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   213→  _wanderTick() {
   214→    // Advance simulated clock each tick
   215→    this.simClock.advance();
   216→
   217→    for (const char of this.characters) {
   218→      if (char.action !== 'idle') continue;
   219→
   220→      // AI mode: delegate to AI decision engine
   221→      if (this.aiMode && this.aiEngine) {
   222→        if (!this.aiEngine.isPending(char.id)) {
   223→          this.aiEngine.tick(char, this.tickIntervalMs);
   224→        }
   225→        continue;
   226→      }
   227→
   228→      // Random wander with cooldown
   229→      char.wanderCooldown -= this.tickIntervalMs;
   230→      if (char.wanderCooldown > 0) continue;
   231→
   232→      // Priority actions first (reactive behavior), then random wander
   233→      const priority = this._getPriorityTarget(char);
   234→      const target = priority ? priority.obj : this._pickWanderTarget(char);
   235→      const action = priority ? priority.action : 'use';
   236→
   237→      if (!target) {
   238→        char.wanderCooldown = 5000;
   239→        continue;
   240→      }
   241→
   242→      const tile = this._findFreeInteractTile(target.position, char);
   243→      if (!tile) {
   244→        char.wanderCooldown = 5000;
   245→        continue;
   246→      }
   247→
   248→      // Already adjacent — interact directly
   249→      const atTarget = tile.tx === Math.round(char.tx) && tile.ty === Math.round(char.ty);
   250→      if (atTarget) {
   251→        this._doInteraction(char, target, action);
   252→        char.wanderCooldown = 30000 + Math.random() * 30000;
   253→      } else if (this._moveCharWithCollision(char, tile.tx, tile.ty)) {
   254→        char.targetObjectId = target.id;
   255→        char.pendingAction = action;
   256→        char.wanderCooldown = 30000 + Math.random() * 30000;
   257→      } else {
   258→        // Path blocked by another character — schedule retry
   259→        char._pendingTarget = { obj: target, action, tile };
   260→        char._retryCount = 1;
   261→        char._retryTimer = 500 + Math.random() * 500;
   262→        char.wanderCooldown = 30000 + Math.random() * 30000;
   263→      }
   264→    }
   265→  }
   266→
   267→  /** Immediate random wander (no cooldown) — used as AI fallback. */
   268→  _randomWander(char) {
   269→    const priority = this._getPriorityTarget(char);
   270→    const target = priority ? priority.obj : this._pickWanderTarget(char);
   271→    const action = priority ? priority.action : 'use';
   272→

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #112
Now I see the issue: 1. Characters start with a `wanderCooldown` of 2000-7000ms (random). 2. The `_wanderTick()` decrements by `tickIntervalMs` (5000ms) per tick, so they should fire on the 1st or 2nd tick. 3. But the stability test was calling `_updateCharacters(500ms)` which doesn't run `_wanderTick()`. The fix is to use `manualTick()` instead. Each tick = 5000ms. For 120 sim-seconds: 24 ticks. With moveSpeed=2 tiles/s, characters can move 10 tiles per tick, which should be fast enough. But I need to verify how the movement system handles large time deltas to make sure it doesn't overshoot or skip tiles.

But the stability test was calling `_updateCharacters(500ms)` which doesn't run `_wanderTick()`.\n\nThe fix is to use `manualTick()` instead. Each tick = 5000ms. For 120 sim-seconds: 24 ticks.\n\n\nWith moveSpeed=2 tiles/s, characters can move 10 tiles per tick, which should be fast enough.    140→    this.interactTimer = 0;
   141→
   142→    // Interaction tracking
   143→    this.lastObjectId = null;
   144→    this.pendingAction = 'use';
   145→    this.interactAnim = null;    // 'reading' | 'sit' | 'phone' | null (uses idle when null)
   146→    this.targetCharId = null;   // ID of a character to face toward upon arrival
   147→
   148→    // Mood system
   149→    this.mood = 'content';       // happy | content | neutral | upset
   150→    this.moodTimer = 0;          // ms until mood resets to content
   151→
   152→    // Collision retry state (managed by SimulationEngine)
   153→    this._pendingTarget = null;  // { obj, action, tile } — blocked goal awaiting retry
   154→    this._retryCount = 0;        // number of failed path attempts for current goal
   155→    this._retryTimer = 0;        // ms until next retry attempt
   156→  }
   157→
   158→  /** Sprite key for the current animation frame. */
   159→  get spriteKey() {
   160→    if (this.action === 'walking') {
   161→      return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;
   162→    }
   163→    if (this.action === 'interacting' && this.interactAnim) {
   164→      return `${this.id}_${this.interactAnim}`;
   165→    }
   166→    return `${this.id}_idle`;
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  get moodEmoji() {
   170→    switch (this.mood) {
   171→      case 'happy': return '😊';
   172→      case 'content': return '😌';
   173→      case 'upset': return '😤';
   174→      default: return null;
   175→    }
   176→  }
   177→
   178→  /** Pixel X position (interpolated between tiles for smooth movement). */
   179→  get px() {
   180→    if (this.path.length === 0) return this.tx * TILE_SIZE;
   181→    const next = this.path[0];
   182→    return (this.prevTx + (next.tx - this.prevTx) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;
   183→  }
   184→
   185→  /** Pixel Y position (interpolated between tiles for smooth movement). */
   186→  get py() {
   187→    if (this.path.length === 0) return this.ty * TILE_SIZE;
   188→    const next = this.path[0];
   189→    return (this.prevTy + (next.ty - this.prevTy) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;
   190→  }
   191→
   192→  /** Start moving toward a goal tile. Returns true if a path was found.
   193→   *  @param {Set|null} extraBlocked - tile keys blocked by other characters.
   194→   */
   195→  moveTo(goalTx, goalTy, extraBlocked = null) {
   196→    const path = findPath(Math.round(this.tx), Math.round(this.ty), goalTx, goalTy, extraBlocked);
   197→    if (path && path.length > 0) {
   198→      this.path = path;
   199→      this.prevTx = Math.round(this.tx);
   200→      this.prevTy = Math.round(this.ty);
   201→      this.moveProgress = 0;
   202→      this.walkFrame = 0;
   203→      this.walkTimer = 0;
   204→      this.action = 'walking';
   205→      this._updateFacing(path[0].tx - this.prevTx, path[0].ty - this.prevTy);
   206→      return true;
   207→    }
   208→    return false;
   209→  }
   210→
   211→  /** Per-frame update: advance position and animation. */
   212→  update(deltaMs) {
   213→    if (this.action === 'walking' && this.path.length > 0) {
   214→      // Walk animation cycle (4 frames)
   215→      this.walkTimer += deltaMs;
   216→      while (this.walkTimer >= this.walkFrameMs) {
   217→        this.walkTimer -= this.walkFrameMs;
   218→        this.walkFrame = (this.walkFrame + 1) % 4;
   219→      }

   220→
   221→      // Advance position along path
   222→      const tileDurationMs = 1000 / this.moveSpeed;
   223→      this.moveProgress += deltaMs / tileDurationMs;
   224→
   225→      while (this.moveProgress >= 1 && this.path.length > 0) {
   226→        this.moveProgress -= 1;
   227→        const arrived = this.path.shift();
   228→        this.tx = arrived.tx;
   229→        this.ty = arrived.ty;
   230→        this.prevTx = arrived.tx;
   231→        this.prevTy = arrived.ty;
   232→
   233→        if (this.path.length > 0) {
   234→          this._updateFacing(this.path[0].tx - this.tx, this.path[0].ty - this.ty);
   235→        }
   236→      }
   237→
   238→      // Arrived at destination
   239→      if (this.path.length === 0) {
   240→        this.moveProgress = 0;
   241→        this.action = 'idle';
   242→      }
   243→    } else if (this.action === 'interacting') {
   244→      this.interactTimer -= deltaMs;
   245→      if (this.interactTimer <= 0) {
   246→        this.action = 'idle';
   247→        this.targetObjectId = null;

   259→    }
   260→  }

   262→  _updateFacing(dx, dy) {
   263→    if (dx > 0) this.facing = 'right';
   264→    else if (dx < 0) this.facing = 'left';
   265→    else if (dy > 0) this.facing = 'down';
   266→    else if (dy < 0) this.facing = 'up';
   267→  }
